commands/module.title: goclarc module
commands/module.description: Generate a typed Clean Architecture module from a YAML schema.

commands/new.title: goclarc new
commands/new.description: Scaffold a new Clean Architecture Go project skeleton.

generated-code.title: Generated Code Guide
generated-code.description: This page explains what each generated file does and what you're expected to modify after generation.

getting-started.title: Getting Started
getting-started.description: This guide walks you through creating a new Go API project and generating your first module from scratch — from zero to running server in under five minutes.

intro.title: What is goclarc?
intro.description: goclarc is a CLI tool that scaffolds production-ready Go APIs following Clean Architecture — similar to what @nestjs/cli does for TypeScript, but for Go.

schema/field-types.title: Field Types
schema/field-types.description: goclarc supports 9 schema types. Each maps to different Go types depending on the database adapter and nullable flag.

schema/overview.title: Schema Overview
schema/overview.description: The schema YAML file is the single source of truth for a module. It defines the domain fields, their types, and constraints — goclarc reads it and generates all Go code consistently.
